expectations.text: The review identifies that domain/order.go imports database/sql, which is a dependency direction violation
expectations.evidence: Report [Important]: 'Domain layer now performs SQL access directly, coupling core domain types to infrastructure.'

expectations.text: The review identifies or questions the FormatPrice export — either flagging it as unnecessarily exported or asking whether it's used outside the service package
expectations.evidence: Report does not flag any unnecessary exports. LoadOrderFromDB, FormatPrice, GetShippingZip are all new exports but none are questioned.

expectations.text: The review identifies the comment 'Called by the OrderHandler in api/handler.go' as encoding upstream caller knowledge that will become stale
expectations.evidence: Refactoring Changes: 'Removed transient call-site comment on FormatPrice' and 'Removed transient call-site comment tied to a specific caller/cache path.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the fmt.Println debug line and either removes it or flags it for removal
expectations.evidence: Refactoring Changes: 'removed debug printline (fmt.Println("formatting price:", cents))'

expectations.text: The review identifies a Law of Demeter concern with method chains reaching through the order's customer/address structure
expectations.evidence: Report does not flag struct field access chains. Same as new skill — test case uses Go struct field access, not method chains.

expectations.text: The report uses the specified severity format (Critical/Important/Minor with file:line, evidence, recommendation)
expectations.evidence: Report uses **[Important]** and **[Minor]** with file:line, Evidence, and Recommendation throughout.
